seoTitle: Darkwood vs Darling Point — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Darkwood and Darling Point. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Darkwood vs Darling Point: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Darkwood and Darling Point.
heading: Darkwood vs Darling Point — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Darkwood, New South Wales and Darling Point, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Darling Point has a lower overall climate risk score (41/100) compared to Darkwood (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Darkwood or Darling Point safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Darkwood has a flood risk score of 23/100, while Darling Point scores 34/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Darkwood scores 56/100 for bushfire risk. Darling Point scores 16/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Darkwood or Darling Point?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Darling Point has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.93% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
